title: A position dependent mass Hamiltonian and abstract ladder operators
authors: Fabio Bagarello, Emanuele Balistreri, Antonino Faddetta
abstract: We consider the Hamiltonian $H$ of a particle in one dimension with a position dependent mass for which we apply the recent strategy of the so-called {\em abstract ladder operators}, in the attempt to find its eigenvalues and eigenvectors. We don't assume that $H$ is self-adjoint, while we focus on the case of a factorizable operator. We show then that pseudo-bosonic operators play a relevant role in this analysis, and we construct bi-coherent states attached to these operators. Explicit examples are discussed.
summary: This paper develops an algebraic method to solve Hamiltonians with position-dependent mass and complex potentials. It demonstrates how pseudo-bosonic structures can be preserved in these systems and provides a complete construction of biorthogonal eigenstates and bi-coherent states.
main_problem: The paper aims to find the eigenvalues, eigenvectors, and bi-coherent states for a class of one-dimensional Hamiltonians featuring position-dependent mass (PDM) and potentially non-self-adjoint potentials.
main_result: The authors successfully constructed biorthogonal eigenstates and proved the mathematical equivalence of two different approaches to defining bi-coherent states using pseudo-bosonic algebra.
method: The study employs an algebraic approach using abstract ladder operators (ALOs), operator factorization, and variable transformations to map the PDM Hamiltonian to a quadratic form.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional particle with a position-dependent mass m(x) and a complex potential V(x), where the Hamiltonian is factorizable and related to pseudo-bosonic operators.
key_observables: Energy eigenvalues (E_n), wavefunctions (eigenstates), and bi-coherent states.
important_parameters: The commutation parameter lambda, the mass function m(x), and the complex parameter gamma; specifically, the real part of lambda determines square-integrability.
main_assumptions: The Hamiltonian is not assumed to be self-adjoint, and the mass function m(x) is assumed to be smooth and positive.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the PDM Hamiltonian and the ALO strategy, proceeds to derive the explicit forms of the potential and operators via factorization, analyzes the square-integrability of states under different mass profiles, and concludes by constructing and proving the equivalence of bi-coherent state definitions.
why_interesting: This work provides a rigorous mathematical framework for handling non-Hermitian systems with spatially varying parameters, which is highly relevant for describing dissipation and effective models in open quantum systems and quantum optics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: |ψ(z; x)|2 (in orange) and |α(z, λ)φ(z1; x)|2 (in blue) for m(x) = m0e−x2. On the top we fix x = 1 and λ = −2 and we plot the functions for zr, zi, and γ = 1 (top left) and γ = 1+2i (top right). Bottom: we plot the functions for x, zi and zR = 3, λR = −2, γ = 1 with λ = −2 (left) and λ = −2 + 2i (right).

Figure 2: |ψ(z; x)|2 (in orange) and |α(z, λ)φ(z1; x)|2 (in blue) for (x, zi) and m(x) = m0 1+x2, with zr = 3, γ = 1 and λR = −2 and different values of λI. On the left λI = 0 (H is self-adjoint). On the right λI = 2 (H ̸= H†).

Figure 3: |ψ(z; x)|2 (in orange) and |α(z, λ)φ(z1; x)|2 (in blue) for (x, zi) and m(x) = m0 1+x2, with zr = 3, γ = 1 and λR = −2 and different values of λI. On the left λI = 0 (H is self-adjoint). On the right λI = 2 (H ̸= H†).
